Operation 'post-updateAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on return values and side effects. Added explicit mention of what is returned (updated account holder information with status) and clarified the partial vs. complete update behavior for better AI agent understanding of operation outcomes.

data.current_description:
Updates the `accountHolderDetails` and `processingTier` of an account holder, and adds bank accounts and shareholders.

When updating `accountHolderDetails`, parameters that are not included in the request are left unchanged except for the following object:

* `metadata`: Updating the metadata replaces the entire object. This means that to update an existing key-value pair, you must provide the changes, as well as other existing key-value pairs.

When updating any field in the following objects, you must submit all the fields required for validation:

 * `address`

* `fullPhoneNumber`
 
* `bankAccountDetails.BankAccountDetail`

* `businessDetails.shareholders.ShareholderContact`
 
 For example, to update the `address.postalCode`, you must also submit the `address.country`, `.city`, `.street`, `.postalCode`, and possibly `.stateOrProvince` so that the address can be validated.

To add a bank account or shareholder, provide the bank account or shareholder details without a `bankAccountUUID` or a `shareholderCode`.
data.description_suggestion: Updates account holder details including accountHolderDetails, processingTier, bank accounts, and shareholders. Partial updates are supported for most fields, but metadata replacement is complete (provide all key-value pairs). When updating address, fullPhoneNumber, bankAccountDetails, or shareholders, all required validation fields must be submitted together. To add new bank accounts or shareholders, omit the bankAccountUUID or shareholderCode. Returns the updated account holder information with status confirmation.
data.reasoning: The current description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on return values and side effects. Added explicit mention of what is returned (updated account holder information with status) and clarified the partial vs. complete update behavior for better AI agent understanding of operation outcomes.

message: Operation 'post-updateAccountHolderState' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary and description are too vague about what states can be modified and what the operation affects. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the dual nature (processing AND payout). Expanded description to clarify input requirements (which state, enabled/disabled), return value (updated status), and side effects (transaction/payout capability impact) for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Disables or enables the processing or payout state of an account holder.
data.summary_suggestion: Enable or disable account holder processing and payout
data.description_suggestion: Modifies the processing or payout state of an account holder by enabling or disabling it. Specify which state to update (processing or payout) and the desired state (enabled or disabled) in the request. Returns the updated account holder status with the new state configuration. This operation affects the account holder's ability to process transactions or receive payouts based on the state modified.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too vague about what states can be modified and what the operation affects. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the dual nature (processing AND payout). Expanded description to clarify input requirements (which state, enabled/disabled), return value (updated status), and side effects (transaction/payout capability impact) for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'post-uploadDocument' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agents. Added specificity about input requirements (account holder identifier, document type), return value (upload confirmation with reference), side effects (document storage for verification), and constraints (format/size requirements). This enables AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

data.current_description: Uploads a document for an account holder. Adyen uses the documents during the [verification process](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/verification-process).
data.description_suggestion: Uploads a verification document for an account holder to support the KYC verification process. The document is stored and used by Adyen during account verification checks. Specify the account holder identifier and document type in the request. Returns confirmation of successful upload with document reference details. Documents must meet format and size requirements as specified in the verification process documentation.
data.reasoning: Current description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agents. Added specificity about input requirements (account holder identifier, document type), return value (upload confirmation with reference), side effects (document storage for verification), and constraints (format/size requirements). This enables AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

message: Operation 'post-closeAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is generic. Description contains useful information but is verbose with markdown links and unclear phrasing. Improved summary is more specific about the status change action. Improved description removes markdown formatting, clarifies the irreversible nature, explains the scope (affects all associated accounts), and explicitly mentions required input (account holder ID) for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Changes the [status of an account holder](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/account-holders-and-accounts#account-holder-statuses) to **Closed**. This state is final. If an account holder is closed, you can't process transactions, pay out funds, or reopen it. If payments are made to an account of an account holder with a **Closed** [`status`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/latest/post/getAccountHolder__resParam_verification-accountHolder-checks-status), the payments are sent to your liable account.
data.summary_suggestion: Set account holder status to Closed
data.description_suggestion: Permanently closes an account holder by setting its status to Closed. This is an irreversible operation. Once closed, you cannot process transactions, pay out funds, or reopen the account holder. All associated accounts are affected. Payments made to accounts of a closed account holder are redirected to your liable account. Requires the account holder ID in the request.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description contains useful information but is verbose with markdown links and unclear phrasing. Improved summary is more specific about the status change action. Improved description removes markdown formatting, clarifies the irreversible nature, explains the scope (affects all associated accounts), and explicitly mentions required input (account holder ID) for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description:
Changes the [status of an account holder](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/account-holders-and-accounts#account-holder-statuses) from **Suspended** to **Inactive**. 
Account holders can have a **Suspended** [`status`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/latest/post/getAccountHolder__resParam_verification-accountHolder-checks-status) if you suspend them through the [`/suspendAccountHolder`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/v5/post/suspendAccountHolder) endpoint or if a verification deadline expires.

You can only unsuspend account holders if they do not have verification checks with a **FAILED** [`status`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/latest/post/getAccountHolder__resParam_verification-accountHolder-checks-status).
data.description_suggestion: Restores a suspended account holder to Inactive status, provided no verification checks have failed. Requires the account holder reference as input. Returns the updated AccountHolderStatus object. This operation can only succeed if the account holder has no failed verification checks. Account holders become suspended either through explicit suspension or when verification deadlines expire.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Current description is verbose and contains implementation details that should be condensed. Enhanced version maintains the critical constraint (no failed verification checks), clarifies the input requirement (account holder reference), specifies the response (AccountHolderStatus), and provides concise context about suspension causes. This improves AI agent interpretability while preserving essential information.
